questions.q: What Windows Event ID detects a new service being installed, and where does it appear?
questions.a: Event 7045 in the System log. It is generated by the Service Control Manager when a new service is registered. Alert when the ImagePath points to user-writable locations like %Temp%, %AppData%, or C:\Users\Public.

questions.q: What three pieces of information does Sysmon Event 1 capture that Windows Event 4688 does not provide in a default configuration?
questions.a: Full command line, parent process image (and parent command line), and SHA256 hash of the executable — all in a single record. Event 4688 requires separate audit policy settings to get command line, provides no hashes, and requires event correlation for parent process information.

questions.q: Explain COM hijacking persistence and why HKCU registration requires no admin rights.
questions.a: COM hijacking plants a malicious DLL as the handler for a COM class used by a legitimate elevated process. HKCU\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID is writable by the current user (no admin required) and is searched before HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID. When an elevated process loads that COM object, it finds the HKCU registration first and loads the attacker's DLL in the elevated process context.

questions.q: Why does detecting Windows persistence require baselining rather than just monitoring known persistence locations?
questions.a: Every autostart location already contains many legitimate entries. Malware adds one more entry with a plausible-looking name. Without a baseline, every entry requires manual investigation. With a baseline of known-good state, detection is a delta check — any new entry not in the baseline triggers an alert. Autoruns with -h (hide Microsoft-signed) reduces noise by filtering trusted entries.

questions.q: What parent-child process chain in Sysmon Event 1 indicates document macro malware, and what confirms lateral movement is imminent?
questions.a: WINWORD.EXE → cmd.exe → powershell.exe (with -enc [base64] argument). Confirmation of imminent lateral movement: Sysmon Event 3 (NetworkConnect) showing the PowerShell process making outbound connections immediately after creation — indicating it has downloaded and is executing a staged payload.
